description: Story A (shared preamble). R2 cross-cutting -- define that action steps are learning-designed interventions, embodied in the step's plain description (recall -> real task -> check -> reflect), never transmission, never labelled. Add to the preamble success-criteria block. Mirror + re-stamp + parity.
acceptance_criteria: In a freshly generated plan, every action step shows four observable elements -- a reference to prior learning/context, a task participants actively do, a check of understanding, and a closing next-step/reflection -- an adversarial reviewer marks all four per step with zero steps failing.
The scan for step-scaffolding labels yields zero occurrences.
Zero action steps are attend/listen/read-only (transmission) -- an adversarial reviewer confirms none.
The dispatched output parses clean with no new fabrication flags.
